assignment_description:
Intro to Acts
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Introducing the New Testament, Notebook

Note: Introducing the New Testament is a resource book that will be used over 5 years. As students get older, they will read more sections and interact with some of the more scholarly questions about the New Testament. Only small sections will be read this year, but feel free to read more.

➜ INTRO
1\. The book of Acts is actually "part two" of the Gospel of Luke, but it has been put in its own section in the New Testament (following the four Gospels) because it is the only book that relates the history of the early church—what happened after the events reported in the Gospels.
2\. Watch this short video about Acts with the author of the book, Introducing the New Testament.
∞ Video Link: Acts

➜ READ & NARRATE
⍞ Materials: Introducing the New Testament
1\. p.205-206 "The book of" - "Lord Jesus Christ."
2\. In the video, the author asked you to pay attention to the key motifs that are outlined in the book of Acts. A motif is a dominant idea or central theme. Read about a couple of the motifs or major themes in Acts:
p.216-220 "The book of" - "is worth remembering."

⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible
3\. Although Luke is the author of two books in the New Testament, he is only mentioned three times in the Bible by name.
Read the following verses to meet the author of the book of Acts: Col. 4:14; 2 Timothy 4:11; Philemon 24; Luke 1:1-4; Acts 1:1-5; Luke 24:36-53

➜ NOTEBOOK
You read about five of the major themes in Acts. Write down each theme on the top line of 5 different pages in your notebook. Then write a brief sentence of what that theme means. As you read through Acts this year, you will add the events that fall under the different themes.

Ascension
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Introducing the New Testament, Notebook

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
Acts 1:8 is the key verse of Acts, and the outline of Acts follows this verse as the gospel spreads and the church grows. Jesus gives the disciples a job after he leaves them on earth to ascend to heaven. "The ascension is the conclusion of the synoptic Gospels, and it is the introduction to Acts, where it begins the story of the development of the church" (Bible Story Handbook, Walton).

➜ READ & NARRATE
⍞ Materials: Introducing the New Testament
1\. p.220-223 "The Jewish matrix" - "of his name."
Read about 3 more major themes in Acts. (You can skip Box 10.5.)

⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible
2\. Acts 1:1-26

➜ NOTEBOOK
Add the three additional themes you read about to your notebook and a brief sentence of explanation for each. Then record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant. There is not one right answer. For example, under the theme Centrality of Jerusalem, you can write: "Jesus orders his disciples not to leave Jerusalem."

Pentecost
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
Acts 2 is considered the church's birthday. Luke shows how the Holy Spirit came upon all the believers there, and how they began speaking in other languages. This may bring to your mind the story from Genesis of the Tower of Babel. However, "[a]t the Tower of Babel, nobody could understand the languages and it drove people apart. At Pentecost, the languages could be understood and it brought people together into a unity that helped start the Church" (Bible Story Handbook).

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 2:1-41
2\. (Optional) Read notes in the ESV Study Bible.

• How does speaking different languages on the Day of Pentecost fit with the mission Jesus gave the disciples in Acts 1:8?

➜ VIEW
Take a moment to look at the painting on p.211 (Fig.10.1 Pentecost). Describe what you see in the painting.

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant. There is not one right answer. You may want to refer to Introducing the New Testament for extra guidance.

Unity
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Introducing the New Testament, Notebook

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
The Holy Spirit came on the Day of Pentecost, and the Christians were united, which led to the expansion of the church in the early days.

➜ READ & NARRATE
⍞ Materials: Introducing the New Testament
1\. p.223-226 "As in the" - "to be offended."
Read about two more major themes in Acts. (Skip the boxes.)

⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible
2\. Acts 2:42-47; Acts 3:1-10
3\. (Optional) Read notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

➜ NOTEBOOK
Add the two additional themes you read about to your notebook and a brief sentence of explanation. Then record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Response to the Healing
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Introducing the New Testament, Notebook

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ READ & NARRATE
⍞ Materials: Introducing the New Testament
1\. p.227-228 "The book of" - "like their master."
Read about one more major theme in Acts.

⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible
2\. Acts 3:11-26

➜ NOTEBOOK
Add the additional theme you read about to your notebook and a brief sentence of explanation. Then record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Brought Before Leadership
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
The healing of the lame man brought the attention of the Jewish leadership to the disciples. The Sadducees mentioned in the passage were "wealthy and politically connected Jews who had control of the temple personnel but were not themselves priests. One of the distinctive elements of their doctrine was the rejection of the resurrection" (Bible Story Handbook).

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 4
2\. (Optional) Read notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

• The word "bold" or "boldness" is repeated in Acts 4. Why was boldness needed on the part of the disciples?

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant. You may want to refer to Introducing the New Testament for guidance.

Ananias and Sapphira
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 4:32-37; Acts 5:1-42
2\. (Optional) Read notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

• How did the offering of Barnabas compare with the offering of Ananias and Sapphira?

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Stephen is Introduced
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
In the book of Acts, Luke periodically gives a "progress report" of the early church. In verse 7, he gives a report that is glowing! But with all these additional people come additional responsibilities that must be taken care of. Chapter 6 will show the movement into the second part of the mission Jesus gave the disciples in Acts 1:8, before he ascended into heaven: that of the gospel moving out from Jerusalem to "all Judea and Samaria."

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 6
2\. (Optional) Read the notes on chapter 6 in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Stephen's Sermon
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
Stephen is charged with blasphemy for speaking "against the law and the temple, both of which had attained a sacred status in Judaism. To speak disrespectfully about them was considered criminal. To suggest that the temple and the law were inadequate or would pass away was heresy. It was taken as strongly as someone today undermining democracy, freedom, or human rights...Stephen's speech showed his respect for...the law, the Tabernacle, and the temple...It was important for Luke to show that Stephen was executed based on false (unsubstantiated) accusations and his association with Jesus" (Bible Story Handbook). While the reports of the church's growth are tremendously encouraging, the story of Stephen's martyrdom showed the persecution the Christians faced and the anger with which the leadership viewed these new believers.

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 7
2\. (Optional) Read the notes of the ESV Concise Study Bible.

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Saul, Philip, Simon, & The Ethiopian Eunuch
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
Through the end of Acts 7, Luke has described the preaching of the gospel in Jerusalem. It had been effective. Thousands had believed. In fact, others had flocked into the city to be with the apostles and had experienced the healings that were taking place. Now, beginning with chapter 8, the gospel expands to Samaria, and Philip becomes the instrument of the first great missionary outreach (James M. Boice, Acts: An Expositional Commentary).

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 8
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Saul's Conversion
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
Saul is brought back into the picture in chapter 9, with the story of his conversion. To the Jewish leaders, "the claim that Jesus is God was a major heresy and a threat to Judaism and had to be eliminated" (Bible Story Handbook). This is exactly what Saul's mission is at the opening of chapter 9. He wanted to persecute and eliminate the people of "The Way." Christians are called this a few times in the New Testament. Do you have an idea why? (Hint: John 14:6)

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 9:1-30
2\. (Optional) Read notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

• What does Saul's testimony teach us about the character of God and who God is?

➜ VIEW
Take a moment to look at the painting on p.218 (Fig.10. The Call of Paul). Describe what you see in the painting. He Qi is a contemporary Christian artist who is influenced by Chinese traditional folk art.

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

• EXTRA HELPING
This interesting article tells how He Qi became a Christian and an artist during a turbulent time in China.
∞ Article Link: Article about artist He Qi

Exam Week

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
Acts
assignment_teachersNote:
★ New Testament: Grades 7-8 — TERM 1 EXAM QUESTIONS
· Tell the story of the early church and how it grew.

· Tell about Peter and a few of the experiences that happened to him as he helped start the early church.

assignment_description:
Peter and Cornelius
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
The end of chapter 9 and chapter 10 focus on Peter. Peter continues to minister, heal, and preach the gospel. In 9:31, Luke gives another progress report on the church. How does he define the early church's health and growth? In chapter 10, the story of Cornelius is a "key turning point in the book as the church is officially opened up to Gentiles" (Bible Story Handbook).

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 9:31-43; Acts 10
2\. (Optional) Read notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

➜ VIEW
Take a moment to look at the painting on p.217 (Fig.10.2 A heavenly revelation). Describe what you see in the painting.

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Peter Tells of His Vision
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
News that Gentiles had also received the word of God and the Holy Spirit spread fast from Caesarea to Jerusalem. It is important to recognize that Jews familiar with their own Scriptures were not put off with the fact that the gospel was proclaimed to the Gentiles or that they shared in the promises of God...The issue that the “circumcised” believers had with Peter’s encounter with the “uncircumcised” is not that they received the word of God from Peter, but the circumstances of the reception. The concern they had was that Peter went into a Gentile home and ate with them; the issue is one of purity. Of course, this was the identical problem Peter had himself before going to Cornelius’s home (Dean Pinter, The Story of God Bible Commentary). The "circumcised" (a sign of the covenant) refers to the Jews, and the "uncircumcised" refers to the Gentiles.

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 11
2\. (Optional) Read notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Peter Freed from Prison
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
In this passage, King Herod is the grandson of Herod who ruled at the time of Jesus' birth. He was new to the throne, so "it is easy to see why this ruler...was interested in gaining favor with the ruling Jewish council" (Bible Story Handbook).

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 12
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Paul Begins His First Missionary Journey
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Introducing the NT, Notebook

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
This week in Spiritual Formation, you will begin an in-depth study of chapter 17 of Acts, which will accompany your reading in Transformed by Truth. As we continue to read Acts, we will gain a larger context for the book and the early church's growth, which helps when studying a specific verse or even a single word.

The focus switches from Peter to the Apostle Paul. It continues to follow the pattern of the key verse, Acts 1:8, as Paul and Barnabas begin a missionary journey that would take them approximately 1,400 miles between AD 46-48 (Bible Story Handbook). Having seen Barnabas' name a few times already in Acts, why do you think he would be a good choice as a missionary?

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 13 (You may want to read and narrate shorter sections.)
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

➜ VIEW
⍞ Materials: Introducing the NT
Look at map 10.1 on p.206. Trace the route of Paul's first missionary journey. Where did most of his travels take place?

➜ NOTEBOOK
Pick one of the locations Paul went to and write a short description of the events that took place there.

Paul and Barnabas Continue to Preach
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
Paul and Barnabas continue on their first missionary journey. What is the gospel message they are preaching? Why do you think the gospel message brings opposition?

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 14 (You may want to read and narrate shorter sections.)
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

• Discuss what 14:22b meant to Paul and Barnabas and what it might mean for you: "Through many tribulations we must enter the kingdom of God."

Jerusalem Council
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
"After Peter declared the universal lordship of Jesus to Cornelius and his household, Luke describes how the early church in Antioch got on with this mission to “the world” by sending out Paul and Barnabas. By all accounts, this first mission journey to the Gentile world was a success. Yet its very success brought the early church to a crossroads. Today, you will read about the Jerusalem Council, which was a crucial moment in the story of the early church. The stakes were high. The decision the early church would make here would shape who they would be (identity) and how they would be together (unity). In this council, the leadership of the early church would deal with the question: What is to be the basis of the inclusion of Gentiles in the newly constituted people of God?" (The Story of the Bible Commentary).

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 15
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Conversion of Lydia
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Introducing the NT, Notebook

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
In Acts 15:36, Paul's second missionary journey begins with Silas, Timothy, and Luke joining him. This second journey would take them approximately 2,800 miles between AD 49-52, with much of their time spent in the city of Corinth (Bible Story Handbook).

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 16
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

➜ VIEW
⍞ Materials: Introducing the NT
Look at map 10.2 on p.207. Trace the route of Paul's second missionary journey. How is this one different from the first?

➜ NOTEBOOK
Pick one of the locations Paul went to and write a short description of the events that took place there.

Paul Speaks to the Areopagus
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
In the context of the entire book of Acts, we have reached chapter 17, the chapter you're studying closely in your Transformed by Truth reading. While you are already familiar with what takes place in this chapter because of your study, reread it in the context of the book of Acts and in the context of Paul's second missionary journey.

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 17
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

Aquila and Priscilla
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Introducing the NT, Notebook

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
Paul continues to be helped in his ministry by acquaintances he meets. In Acts 18, Aquila and Priscilla help encourage Paul and minister alongside him, and later in the chapter, they kindly teach Apollos. The flow of the book of Acts shows the growth of the early church and shows how these individuals (people like Lydia, Aquila, Priscilla, Luke, etc.) were used by God to help build the church. Interestingly, Gallio mentioned here is the brother of Seneca, a (still to this day) famous Stoic philosopher. (Remember Paul's message in chapter 17 specifically included Stoicism when he preached against the Greeks and their philosophy.) Paul's third missionary journey begins in Acts 18:22 (some lists may say verse 23).

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 18
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

• What does the involvement of individuals of all backgrounds in the church's growth tell us about God and his character and who he is?

➜ VIEW
⍞ Materials: Introducing the NT
Look at map 10.3 on p.208. Trace the route of Paul's third missionary journey. Is the route more similar to the first or the second journey?

➜ NOTEBOOK
Pick one of the locations Paul went to and write a short description of the events that took place there.

Paul Against the Goddess Diana
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
Paul's third missionary journey begins in chapter 18, covering approximately 2,700 miles from 53-57 AD. More than half of his time would be spent in Ephesus.

➜ VIEW
Look at figure 10.4 Ephesus on p. 220. Ephesus was the third largest city at that time and home of one of the seven wonders of the ancient world–the temple of Artemis (Diana), the goddess of the hunt.

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 19
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

Catch Up

➜ CATCH UP
Use this time to catch up on lessons in this or another course, or explore Extra Helpings.

Exam Week

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
Acts
assignment_teachersNote:
★ New Testament: Grades 7-8 — TERM 2 EXAM QUESTIONS
· Tell about Paul's first missionary journey.

· Name three people who helped support Paul in his ministry in your reading this term.

assignment_description:
Eutychus Falls Asleep
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
In Acts 20, the story of Eutychus stands out as an interesting story of Paul healing a young man who fell out of a window...while listening to Paul speak! The presence of many lamps was not usual, but it gave the idea that the meeting had been planned to go into the night. "Possibly the heat or even odor from the lamps contributed to Eutychus seeking a window seat. Glass was rare in windows. Some windows could be large, especially above the ground floor" (Cultural Backgrounds Study Bible).

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 20
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

• What are your thoughts about Paul's final message to the Ephesian elders? What does the emotional reaction on both sides tell you about their relationship?

➜ NOTEBOOK
Pick one of the locations Paul went to and write a short description of the events that took place there.

Paul Goes to Jerusalem
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
The missionary journeys are completed, Paul is going to Jerusalem for the last time, and arrives there in this chapter. He is arrested and so far as we know never again finds freedom.

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 21:1-36
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

• How does this passage show the progress of the spread of the early church in obedience to Acts 1:8?

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Paul Speaks to the People
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
"Paul recounted his Jewish background and training as a Pharisee, his zeal in persecuting Christians, and his experience on the road to Damascus. He did not refer explicitly to his status as a Christian, for that was not a choice against his Jewish identity. The crowd was enraged when he told them of his call to preach to the Gentiles, for a Jew to recognize Jesus as the Messiah was not as objectionable as a Jew who reduced or eliminated boundaries between Jew and Gentile. The next day in the Sanhedrin, neither the identity of Jesus nor the mission to the Gentiles caused problems; it was the question of the resurrection" (Bible Story Handbook).

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 21:37-40; Acts 22
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Paul Before the Council
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
Do you think of circumstances as being things that are against you—something that God cannot control? Have you ever found yourself thinking, “If the circumstances of my life were different, perhaps then I could have been somebody or could have done something great for God or could have triumphed in the particular difficulty in which I am now”? Do not think that way. Circumstances do not limit God. Circumstances are not independent of God. God creates circumstances. God is the master of circumstances (Acts: An Expositional Commentary). In this chapter, we will see how God uses an interesting circumstance to save Paul from being murdered.

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 23
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Paul Before Felix
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
"In this portion of the trial, Paul was accused of being a troublemaker who stirred up riots as a leader of the Christians. His opponents added the charge of desecrating the temple. Paul denied the charges, but did identify himself as a Jew and follower of the Way" (Bible Story Handbook).

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 24
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

• Why do you think Felix brings his wife to visit Paul and listen to him talk about the gospel?

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Paul Before Agrippa
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
"Festus succeeded Felix and heard the same charges that his predecessor had heard two years earlier. Paul knew that Festus was currying favor with the Jews and that he might be inclined to turn Paul over to them, which would have been catastrophic. As a Roman citizen, Paul had a right to appeal to Caesar, and he did so" (Bible Story Handbook).

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 25
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

• What do you think about Festus' response of not having anything by which to charge Paul?

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Paul Defends Himself to Agrippa
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
"Nothing [in Paul's testimony] indicates that he was making proselytes to Judaism, thus indicating that this message had nothing to do with Jewish law. Agrippa was astonished at the claim when he referenced Christ and his resurrection. Despite that, he was persuaded that the charges against Paul by the Jews were unfounded. This finding is very important to the case Luke has been making throughout the book" (Bible Story Handbook).

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 26
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

• What do you think Luke's purpose is in presenting these different trials where Paul is put on the defense of himself?

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Shipwreck
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook, Introducing the New Testament (optional)

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
After all the trials he has been through, Paul is on his way to Rome. Unfortunately, he faces a different kind of difficulty this time. However, through it all, "God preserved Paul because he still had work for him to do" (Bible Story Handbook).

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 27
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

➜ NOTEBOOK
Record anything from today's reading under any of the themes that might be relevant.

Paul in Rome
⍞ Materials: ESV Concise Study Bible, Notebook

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time about the Book of Acts?

➜ INTRO
"God is at work getting his servant, Paul, to Rome where he will share the good news of Jesus" (Bible Story Handbook).

➜ READ & NARRATE
1\. Acts 28
2\. (Optional) Read the notes in the ESV Concise Study Bible.

• How has the gospel message gone into the world over the events of the book of Acts?

Review
⍞ Materials: Introducing the NT, Notebook

➜ READ
1\. Read Overview of Acts and recall everything you read about this year. p.206-209
2\. Read Box 10.7 Church of Joy p. 226. What does it mean to have joy, and why is that an important characteristic for Christians?
3\. Read Conclusion p.228.

➜ NOTEBOOK
Look at the different themes in your notebook. Which theme(s) has the most events associated with it?

➜ VIEW
Watch this video about the last chapters of the book of Acts.
∞ Video Link: Bound for Rome: Acts 21-28

• How did the gospel reach you and your family? How are you continuing to spread it to others?

Catch Up

➜ CATCH UP
Use this time to catch up on lessons in this or another course, or explore Extra Helpings.

EXTRA HELPINGS
You may be interested in watching this movie on the Book of Acts. They use the exact words of the Bible.
∞ Video Link: Movie on the Book of Acts

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course.
